#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Generate and initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
AIDE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_DB" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AIDE_DB")"
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz "$AIDE_DB"

# Set correct permissions for the AIDE database
chown root:root "$AIDE_DB"
chmod 600 "$AIDE_DB"

#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit tools to be monitored by AIDE
audit_tools=(
    /usr/bin/ausearch
    /usr/sbin/aureport
    /sbin/auditctl
    /sbin/audispd
    /sbin/auditd
)

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file
sudo cp -n /etc/aide.conf /etc/aide.conf.bak

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration
update_aide_config() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    if ! grep -q "^$tool_path" /etc/aide.conf; then
        echo "$tool_path R, p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+t" | sudo tee -a /etc/aide.conf > /dev/null
    fi
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE for monitoring
for tool in "${audit_tools[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then
        update_aide_config "$tool"
    else
        echo "Warning: $tool does not exist and will not be monitored by AIDE."
    fi
done

# Update the AIDE database with the new configuration
sudo aide --init

# Ensure AIDE is set to run on boot
if ! systemctl is-enabled aidecheck.service &> /dev/null; then
    sudo systemctl enable aidecheck.service
fi

echo "AIDE has been configured to monitor audit tools."

if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then

EOF

    sudo yum install -y mailx
fi

# Configure mail to use the local sendmail service
if [ ! -f /etc/mail.rc ]; then
    cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/mail.rc
set sendmail=/usr/sbin/sendmail
EOF
fi

echo "AIDE configuration for email reporting is complete."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Define the path to the AIDE configuration file
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Create a backup of the original AIDE configuration file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Function to add ACL verification to a ruleset
add_acl_to_ruleset() {
    local ruleset="$1"
    # Check if the ruleset already includes ACL verification
    if grep -q "^$ruleset.*acl$" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
        echo "ACL verification is already enabled for $ruleset."
    else
        # Add ACL verification to the ruleset
        sed -i "/^$ruleset/s/$/ acl/" "$AIDE_CONF"
        echo "Added ACL verification to $ruleset."
    fi
}

# List of common AIDE rulesets to modify
RULESETS=("ALL" "BINARIES" "TEXT" "SCRIPTS" "CONFIG")

# Apply ACL verification to each ruleset
for RULESET in "${RULESETS[@]}"; do
    add_acl_to_ruleset "$RULESET"
done

# Reload AIDE configuration
aide --init > /dev/null 2>&1

echo "AIDE configuration updated to include ACL verification for all specified rulesets."

    echo "Failed to update GRUB configuration file."
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GnuTLS honors system-wide cryptographic policy
# This script configures GnuTLS to use the system crypto policy
# and disables legacy/insecure protocol versions.

# Define the path to the GnuTLS configuration file
GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"

# Check if the GnuTLS configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]]; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration file not found: $GNUTLS_CONF"
  exit 1
fi

# Define the required settings for the GnuTLS configuration
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
  "DEFAULT_PRIORITY = \"SYSTEM\""
)

# Function to check if a setting is already present in the configuration file
setting_exists() {
  local setting="$1"
  grep -q "^$setting" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
}

# Apply the required settings
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
  if ! setting_exists "$setting"; then
    echo "Adding setting to $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
    echo "$setting" >> "$GNUTLS_CONF"
  else
    echo "Setting already exists in $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
  fi
done

# Ensure the GnuTLS configuration file is owned by root and has the correct permissions
chown root:root "$GNUTLS_CONF"
chmod 644 "$GNUTLS_CONF"

# Reload the GnuTLS service to apply changes
systemctl reload gnutls-serv || true

echo "GnuTLS configuration has been updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the GNOME settings daemon is installed
if ! rpm -q gnome-settings-daemon; then
    echo "gnome-settings-daemon is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Create or update the dconf profile for system-wide settings
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/gdm"
echo "user-db:user" > "$DCONF_PROFILE"
echo "system-db:local" >> "$DCONF_PROFILE"

# Create the dconf local database directory if it doesn't exist
DCONF_LOCAL_DB="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCAL_DB"

# Define the dconf settings to lock the screen on smartcard removal
DCONF_SETTINGS="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_SETTINGS"
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0

[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
logout=''
power-off=''
suspend=''
hibernate=''
EOF

# Update the dconf database
dconf update

# Ensure the settings are enforced by locking down the keys
DCONF_LOCK="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/locks"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCK"

cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_LOCK/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/power-off
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/suspend
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/hibernate
EOF

# Update the dconf database again to apply locks
dconf update

echo "System-wide policy set to lock the screen on smartcard removal."

# Ensure the GPG signature verification is enabled for local package installations

# Check if the RPM configuration file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/rpm/macros ]; then
    echo "RPM macros file not found at /etc/rpm/macros"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the required macro to enable GPG signature verification
REQUIRED_MACRO="%_gpgcheck 1"

# Check if the required macro is already set in the RPM configuration file
if grep -q "^%_gpgcheck" /etc/rpm/macros; then
    # If the macro exists, check if it's set to 1 (enabled)

    if [ "$CURRENT_VALUE" -ne 1 ]; then

        sed -i "s/^%_gpgcheck.*/$REQUIRED_MACRO/" /etc/rpm/macros

    # If the macro does not exist, append it to the file
    echo "$REQUIRED_MACRO" >> /etc/rpm/macros
fi

# Verify that the GPG signature verification is now enabled
if grep -q "^%_gpgcheck 1" /etc/rpm/macros; then
    echo "GPG signature verification for local package installations is enabled."
else
    echo "Failed to enable GPG signature verification for local package installations."

REQUIRED_MACRO="%_gpgcheck 1"

# Check if the required macro is already set in the RPM configuration file
if grep -q "^%_gpgcheck" /etc/rpm/macros; then
    # If the macro exists, check if it's set to 1 (enabled)
    CURRENT_VALUE=$(grep "^%_gpgcheck" /etc/rpm/macros | awk '{print $2}')
    if [ "$CURRENT_VALUE" -ne 1 ]; then
        # If not enabled, update the value
        sed -i "s/^%_gpgcheck.*/$REQUIRED_MACRO/" /etc/rpm/macros
    fi
else
    # If the macro does not exist, append it to the file
    echo "$REQUIRED_MACRO" >> /etc/rpm/macros
fi

# Verify that the GPG signature verification is now enabled
if grep -q "^%_gpgcheck 1" /etc/rpm/macros; then
    echo "GPG signature verification for local package installations is enabled."
else
    echo "Failed to enable GPG signature verification for local package installations."
    exit 1
fi
```

# Ensure PAM configuration for root account lockout is set
pam_config="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
lockout_line="auth required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail deny=3 unlock_time=600"

if ! grep -q "^$lockout_line$" "$pam_config"; then
    # Add the lockout line if it doesn't exist

fi

    # Add the lockout line if it doesn't exist
    sed -i "/^auth.*required.*pam_unix\.so/a $lockout_line" "$password_auth_config"
fi

# Ensure tally2 database is reset for root
tally2_reset() {
    local username="$1"
    if getent passwd "$username" >/dev/null; then
        pam_tally2 --user="$username" --reset
    fi
}

tally2_reset "root"

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

authconfig --enablefaillock --faillockargs="deny=3 unlock_time=900" --updateall

# Ensure the configuration persists across reboots by modifying /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth
if ! grep -q "pam_tally2.so deny=3 unlock_time=900" /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
    sed -i '/^auth.*required.*pam_unix.so.*/a auth        required      pam_tally2.so deny=3 unlock_time=900' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
fi

if ! grep -q "pam_tally2.so deny=3 unlock_time=900" /etc/pam.d/password-auth; then
    sed -i '/^auth.*required.*pam_unix.so.*/a auth        required      pam_tally2.so deny=3 unlock_time=900' /etc/pam.d/password-auth
fi

# Ensure the lockout mechanism is applied to all users by modifying /etc/security/faillock.conf
if ! grep -q "unlock_time = 900" /etc/security/faillock.conf; then
    echo "unlock_time = 900" >> /etc/security/faillock.conf
fi

# Restart the system-auth service to apply changes
systemctl restart systemd-logind.service
```

    local value="$2"
    
    # Check if the key already exists in the file
    if grep -q "^$key" "$PAM_CONFIG"; then
        # Update the existing key with the new value
        sed -i "s/^$key.*/$key = $value/" "$PAM_CONFIG"

# Define the desired permissions
desired_permissions="0740"

# Function to apply permissions recursively to dot-files in a directory
apply_permissions() {
    local dir="$1"
    find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name ".*" ! -name "." \) -exec chmod "$desired_permissions" {} +
}

# Apply permissions to root's home directory
if [ -d /root ]; then
    apply_permissions /root
fi

# Iterate over all users with a home directory
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid gid comment home shell; do
    # Skip system accounts (UID < 1000) and non-existent home directories
    if [ "$uid" -ge 1000 ] && [ -d "$home" ]; then
        apply_permissions "$home"
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

    local dir="$1"

}

if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Please run this script as root"
    exit 1

# Function to enable Kernel Page-Table Isolation (KPTI) in GRUB configuration
enable_kpti() {
    local grub_cfg="/etc/default/grub"

    # Check if the KPTI parameter is already set
    if grep -q "^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$grub_cfg" && ! grep -q "pti=on" "$grub_cfg"; then
        # Append pti=on to the existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line
        sed -i '/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/"$/ pti=on"/' "$grub_cfg"
    elif ! grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$grub_cfg"; then
        # Add a new GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line with pti=on if it doesn't exist
        echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="pti=on"' >> "$grub_cfg"
    fi

    # Update the GRUB configuration to apply changes
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
}

# Enable KPTI for all currently installed kernels and future kernels
enable_kpti

# Verify that KPTI is enabled in the current kernel
if ! cat /proc/cmdline | grep -q "pti=on"; then
    echo "Warning: KPTI is not enabled in the current running kernel. Reboot to apply changes."
else
    echo "KPTI is successfully enabled for all kernels."
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the kernel parameter to disable legacy virtual syscalls
PARAMETER="vsyscall=none"

# Function to update GRUB configuration for a specific kernel version
update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local KERNEL_VERSION="$1"
    local GRUB_CONFIG="/etc/default/grub.d/90-vsyscall-disable.cfg"

    # Create or update the GRUB configuration file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$GRUB_CONFIG" ]; then
        echo "Creating GRUB configuration file: $GRUB_CONFIG"
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$GRUB_CONFIG")"
        cat <<EOF > "$GRUB_CONFIG"
# Disable legacy virtual syscalls for kernel $KERNEL_VERSION
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX+=" vsyscall=none "
EOF
    fi

    # Update the GRUB configuration for the specific kernel version
    echo "Updating GRUB configuration for kernel: $KERNEL_VERSION"
    grub2-mkconfig -o "/boot/grub2/grub.cfg.$KERNEL_VERSION"
}

# Get a list of all installed kernels
INSTALLED_KERNELS=$(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel)

# Update GRUB configuration for each installed kernel
for KERNEL in $INSTALLED_KERNELS; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$KERNEL"
done

# Ensure the setting persists across reboots by updating the default GRUB configuration
echo "Updating default GRUB configuration to persist vsyscall=none"
sed -i "/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/\"$/ $PARAMETER\"/" /etc/default/grub
grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

# Inform the user that the script has completed successfully
echo "Legacy virtual syscall capability has been disabled for all installed kernels and will persist across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y rsyslog
fi

# Enable and start rsyslog service
systemctl enable rsyslog
systemctl start rsyslog

# Configure TLS for log forwarding
TLS_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d/tls"
mkdir -p "$TLS_DIR"

# Generate self-signed certificates if they do not exist
if [ ! -f "$TLS_DIR/ca.crt" ] || [ ! -f "$TLS_DIR/client.crt" ] || [ ! -f "$TLS_DIR/client.key" ]; then
    openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 \
        -keyout "$TLS_DIR/ca.key" -out "$TLS_DIR/ca.crt" \
        -subj "/CN=CA"
    
    openssl req -new -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 \
        -keyout "$TLS_DIR/client.key" -out "$TLS_DIR/client.csr" \
        -subj "/CN=Client"
    
    openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in "$TLS_DIR/client.csr" \
        -CA "$TLS_DIR/ca.crt" -CAkey "$TLS_DIR/ca.key" -CAcreateserial \
        -out "$TLS_DIR/client.crt"
fi

# Configure rsyslog to use TLS
cat <<EOF > /etc/rsyslog.d/60-remote-forward.conf
\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1
\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name
\$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer *
\$ActionSendStreamDriverCAFile $TLS_DIR/ca.crt
\$ActionSendStreamDriverCertFile $TLS_DIR/client.crt
\$ActionSendStreamDriverKeyFile $TLS_DIR/client.key

*.* @@remote-collector:6514;RSYSLOG_SyslogProtocol23Format
EOF

# Restart rsyslog to apply changes
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Verify the configuration by checking if the files exist and have the correct permissions
if [[ ! -f /var/log/secure || ! -f /var/log/messages ]]; then
    echo "Log files not created. Please check rsyslog configuration."
    exit 1
fi

# Set appropriate permissions for log files
chmod 640 /var/log/secure /var/log/messages
chown root:root /var/log/secure /var/log/messages

echo "Logging configuration has been successfully set up and verified."
```

        echo "$key = $value" >> /etc/sysctl.conf

# Ensure FireWire kernel modules are blacklisted
if ! grep -q "blacklist firewire-core" /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf; then
    echo "blacklist firewire-core" | sudo tee -a /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf > /dev/null
fi

if ! grep -q "blacklist firewire-ohci" /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf; then
    echo "blacklist firewire-ohci" | sudo tee -a /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf > /dev/null
fi

if ! grep -q "blacklist firewire-sbp2" /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf; then
    echo "blacklist firewire-sbp2" | sudo tee -a /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-firewire.conf > /dev/null
fi

# Remove any existing FireWire modules that might be loaded
sudo rmmod firewire-core 2>/dev/null
sudo rmmod firewire-ohci 2>/dev/null
sudo rmmod firewire-sbp2 2>/dev/null

sudo systemctl restart systemd-modules-load.service

# Function to check if a directory is on a separate filesystem
is_on_separate_fs() {
    local dir="$1"
    df "$dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}' | grep -qE '^/dev/'
}

# Check if /boot is on a separate filesystem
if is_on_separate_fs "/boot"; then
    # Get the UUID of the boot partition
    BOOT_UUID=$(df /boot | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}')
    
    # Check if the noexec option is already set in /etc/fstab

        # Add noexec option to /etc/fstab
        sed -i "/^UUID=$BOOT_UUID/ s/$/,noexec/" /etc/fstab

    # Remount the boot partition with the noexec option
    mount -o remount,noexec /boot
else
    echo "Boot partition is not a separately mounted filesystem. No action needed."
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Function to check if a directory is on a separate filesystem
is_on_separate_fs() {
    local dir="$1"
    df "$dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}' | grep -qE '^/dev/'
}

# Check if /boot is on a separate filesystem
if is_on_separate_fs "/boot"; then
    # Get the UUID of the boot partition
    BOOT_UUID=$(df /boot | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}')
    
    # Check if the noexec option is already set in /etc/fstab
    if ! grep -qE "^UUID=$BOOT_UUID.*noexec" /etc/fstab; then
        # Add noexec option to /etc/fstab
        sed -i "/^UUID=$BOOT_UUID/ s/$/,noexec/" /etc/fstab
    fi
    
    # Remount the boot partition with the noexec option
    mount -o remount,noexec /boot
else
    echo "Boot partition is not a separately mounted filesystem. No action needed."
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define the group for privileged users
PRIVILEGED_GROUP="wheel"

# Check if the group exists, create it if not
getent group "$PRIVILEGED_GROUP" > /dev/null 2>&1 || groupadd "$PRIVILEGED_GROUP"

# Change ownership of /dev/kmsg to root:privileged_group
chown root:"$PRIVILEGED_GROUP" /dev/kmsg

# Set permissions on /dev/kmsg to allow read access only for the privileged group
chmod g+r,o-r /dev/kmsg

# Ensure that the changes persist across reboots by adding a udev rule
cat <<EOF | tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-kmsg-permissions.rules > /dev/null
KERNEL=="kmsg", GROUP="$PRIVILEGED_GROUP", MODE="640"
EOF

# Reload udev rules to apply the changes immediately
udevadm control --reload-rules && udevadm trigger

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Disable kernel image loading capability at runtime
echo 1 > /proc/sys/kernel/kexec_load_disabled

if ! grep -q "^kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1" /etc/sysctl.conf; then
    echo "kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.conf
fi

# Apply the changes immediately
sysctl -p
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define the kernel parameter to enable free-page poisoning
KERNEL_PARAM="page_poison=1"

# Function to update GRUB configuration for all installed kernels
update_grub_for_all_kernels() {
    # Get a list of all installed kernels
    KERNELS=$(grubby --info=ALL | grep ^kernel | awk -F= '{print $2}')

    # Iterate over each kernel and update its GRUB entry
    for K in $KERNELS; do
        # Check if the parameter is already set
        if ! grubby --info="$K" | grep -q "$KERNEL_PARAM"; then
            # Append the parameter to the existing GRUB command line
            grubby --update-kernel="$K" --args="$KERNEL_PARAM"
        fi
    done
}

# Function to ensure the parameter is set for future kernels
ensure_future_kernels() {
    # Get the current default kernel parameters
    DEFAULT_PARAMS=$(grubby --default-kernel | xargs grubby --info | grep ^args | awk -F= '{print $2}')

    # Check if the parameter is already included in the default parameters
    if [[ ! "$DEFAULT_PARAMS" =~ "$KERNEL_PARAM" ]]; then
        # Append the parameter to the default GRUB command line
        grubby --update-kernel="$(grubby --default-kernel)" --args="$DEFAULT_PARAMS $KERNEL_PARAM"
    fi
}

# Update GRUB for all currently installed kernels
update_grub_for_all_kernels

# Ensure the parameter is set for future kernels
ensure_future_kernels

# Update GRUB configuration to apply changes
grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure SELinux is installed and enabled
if ! rpm -q selinux-policy-devel &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y selinux-policy-devel
fi

# Set SELinux to enforcing mode
setenforce 1
sed -i 's/^SELINUX=.*$/SELINUX=enforcing/' /etc/selinux/config

# Install the necessary packages for AppArmor
if ! rpm -q apparmor &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y apparmor apparmor-utils
fi

# Enable and start the AppArmor service
systemctl enable apparmor
systemctl start apparmor

# Set AppArmor to enforce mode
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/*

# Create a default deny policy for all binaries
echo "profile /bin/* flags=(attach_disconnected) { deny exec /bin/*; }" > /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny
echo "profile /usr/bin/* flags=(attach_disconnected) { deny exec /usr/bin/*; }" >> /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny

# Load the new policy
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny

# Ensure the policy is reloaded on reboot
echo "/sbin/aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny" >> /etc/rc.local
chmod +x /etc/rc.local

# Allow specific trusted binaries by creating exceptions
function allow_binary {
    local binary_path=$1
    echo "profile $binary_path flags=(attach_disconnected) { allow exec $binary_path; }" > "/etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.$(basename $binary_path)"
    aa-enforce "/etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.$(basename $binary_path)"
}

# Example: Allow the 'ls' command
allow_binary /bin/ls

# Example: Allow the 'cat' command
allow_binary /bin/cat

# Reload all AppArmor profiles to apply changes
aa-complain /etc/apparmor.d/*
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/*

# Verify that AppArmor is enforcing
aa-status | grep "mode = enforcing"

# Set AppArmor to enforce mode
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/*

# Create a default deny policy for all binaries
echo "profile /bin/* flags=(attach_disconnected) { deny exec /bin/*; }" > /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny
echo "profile /usr/bin/* flags=(attach_disconnected) { deny exec /usr/bin/*; }" >> /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny

# Load the new policy
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny

# Ensure the policy is reloaded on reboot
echo "/sbin/aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.all_binaries_deny" >> /etc/rc.local
chmod +x /etc/rc.local

# Allow specific trusted binaries by creating exceptions
function allow_binary {
    local binary_path=$1
    echo "profile $binary_path flags=(attach_disconnected) { allow exec $binary_path; }" > "/etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.$(basename $binary_path)"
    aa-enforce "/etc/apparmor.d/usr.bin.$(basename $binary_path)"
}

# Example: Allow the 'ls' command
allow_binary /bin/ls

# Example: Allow the 'cat' command
allow_binary /bin/cat

# Reload all AppArmor profiles to apply changes
aa-complain /etc/apparmor.d/*
aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/*

# Verify that AppArmor is enforcing
aa-status | grep "mode = enforcing"
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure SSH server configuration is idempotent by checking and setting necessary permissions

# Function to check and set ownership and permissions for user's home directory and SSH-related files
check_and_set_permissions() {
    local user_home=$(eval echo ~$1)
    local ssh_dir="$user_home/.ssh"
    
    # Check if the user's home directory exists
    if [ -d "$user_home" ]; then
        # Ensure the home directory is owned by the user and not world-writable
        chown $1:$1 "$user_home"
        chmod 750 "$user_home"
        
        # Check if the .ssh directory exists
        if [ -d "$ssh_dir" ]; then
            # Ensure the .ssh directory is owned by the user and not group or world-writable
            chown $1:$1 "$ssh_dir"
            chmod 700 "$ssh_dir"
            
            # Ensure all files in .ssh are owned by the user and not group or world-writable
            find "$ssh_dir" -type f -exec chown $1:$1 {} \;
            find "$ssh_dir" -type f -exec chmod 600 {} \;
        fi
    fi
}

# Apply permissions checks to all users with a home directory
for user in $(ls /home); do
    check_and_set_permissions "$user"
done

# Ensure the SSH daemon configuration is set to enforce strict ownership and permissions checks
sshd_config="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Backup the original sshd_config file if it hasn't been backed up yet
if [ ! -f "${sshd_config}.bak" ]; then
    cp "$sshd_config" "${sshd_config}.bak"
fi

# Ensure the following settings are present in the sshd_config file
settings=(
    "StrictModes yes"
    "PermitRootLogin no"
    "PasswordAuthentication no"
)

for setting in "${settings[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -q "^$setting" "$sshd_config"; then
        echo "$setting" >> "$sshd_config"
    fi
done

# Restart the SSH daemon to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

done

# Ensure the SSH daemon configuration is set to enforce strict ownership and permissions checks
sshd_config="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Backup the original sshd_config file if it hasn't been backed up yet
if [ ! -f "${sshd_config}.bak" ]; then
    cp "$sshd_config" "${sshd_config}.bak"
fi

# Ensure the following settings are present in the sshd_config file
settings=(
    "StrictModes yes"
    "PermitRootLogin no"
    "PasswordAuthentication no"
)

for setting in "${settings[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -q "^$setting" "$sshd_config"; then
        echo "$setting" >> "$sshd_config"
    fi
done

# Restart the SSH daemon to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd
```

    echo "library: libsoftokn3.so" >> /etc/pkcs11/modules/localdb.module

    certutil -A -n "YourCAName" -t "CT,C,C" -i /path/to/your/ca.crt -d sql:/etc/pki/nssdb
fi

systemctl restart sshd

echo "Smart card authentication configuration completed."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure USBGuard is installed
if ! rpm -q usbguard &> /dev/null; then
    echo "USBGuard is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Generate a policy file based on currently connected devices
POLICY_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
TEMP_POLICY_FILE=$(mktemp)

# Allow all currently connected USB devices
usbguard list-devices --output-script > "$TEMP_POLICY_FILE"

# Ensure the policy file exists and is writable
touch "$POLICY_FILE"
chmod 640 "$POLICY_FILE"

# Replace the existing policy with the new one
mv "$TEMP_POLICY_FILE" "$POLICY_FILE"

# Reload USBGuard to apply the new policy
usbguard reload

# Enable USBGuard service to persist across reboots
systemctl enable usbguard.service

echo "USB device authorization policy has been updated and will persist across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Define audit rules for successful mount operations by regular users
audit_rules=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
)

# Function to add audit rules if they do not already exist
add_audit_rules() {
    for rule in "${audit_rules[@]}"; do
        if ! grep -q "^$rule$" /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; then
            echo "$rule" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/export.rules
        fi
    done
}

# Function to reload audit daemon configuration
reload_auditd() {
    augenrules --load

        echo "$audit_rule" | sudo tee -a /etc/audit/rules.d/lastlog.rules > /dev/null
        sudo augenrules --load
    fi
}

# Function to add audit rule using auditctl
add_audit_rule_auditctl() {
    if ! sudo auditctl -l | grep -q "$audit_rule"; then
        echo "$audit_rule" | sudo tee -a /etc/audit/rules.d/lastlog.rules > /dev/null
        sudo service auditd restart
    fi
}

# Check which mechanism is in use and add the rule accordingly
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    add_audit_rule_augenrules
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    add_audit_rule_auditctl
fi
```

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit log directory
AUDIT_LOG_DIR="/var/log/audit"

# Define the threshold for low disk space (25%)
LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD=25

# Function to check and set the auditd low-space action
configure_auditd_low_space() {
    # Check if the auditd configuration file exists
    if [ ! -f /etc/audit/auditd.conf ]; then
        echo "Auditd configuration file not found."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Get the current disk usage percentage of the audit log directory
    current_usage=$(df --output=pcent "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -n 1 | sed 's/%//')

    # Check if the current usage is above or equal to the threshold
    if [ "$current_usage" -ge "$LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD" ]; then
        echo "Audit log directory is already at or above the low space threshold."
    else
        # Set the action to take when disk space falls below 25%
        sed -i '/^space_left_action/s/=.*/=syslog/' /etc/audit/auditd.conf
        sed -i '/^action_mail_acct/s/=.*/=root/' /etc/audit/auditd.conf

        # Restart auditd to apply changes
        systemctl restart auditd

        echo "Auditd configured to warn administrators when disk space falls below 25%."
    fi
}

# Ensure the script is idempotent by checking current configuration
current_action=$(grep '^space_left_action' /etc/audit/auditd.conf | cut -d'=' -f2)
if [ "$current_action" != "syslog" ]; then
    configure_auditd_low_space
else
    echo "Auditd is already configured to warn administrators when disk space falls below 25%."
fi

# Ensure the configuration persists across reboots by enabling auditd service
systemctl enable auditd
